ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey and Lawrence Siracusa for Successor Trustee Matthew Woodbury; Daniel Conrad for trust beneficiaries Colleen Bordelon and Thomas McGhee; Allison Bellumori for assignee Brandenburger and Davis.

Mr. McCaffrey stated that the objectors had raised several challenges to the petition which discovery showed to be without basis, and that ther was good cause to bring a motion for summary judgment. Mr. Conrad stated that in his opinion the motion for summary judgment was well taken and should have been granted. He added that the trust beneficiaries had no objection to the award of attorneys' fees which had been requested. Mr. Siracusa concurred with Mr. Conrad, and stated that he had no objection to the fee request. Ms. Bellumori stated that her client had no objection to the fees requested by Mr. McCaffrey, and requested that the Court authorize payment of the fees requested by Brandenburger and Davis as well. Mr. Conrad acknowledged that both trust beneficiaries had moved and were difficult to locate, and had benefitted from the heir-finding services of Brandenburger and Davis, and had no objection to their fees under the assignements previously agreed to by them. In response to an inquiry from the Court, Ms. Bellumori agreed to file a supplemental submission on behalf of Brandenburger and Davis within the next two weeks. The Court allowed two weeks for filing of the supplemental submission, with the matter to be deemed submitted as of June 26, 2017.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hannah Salassi for petitioner Charles Watts; Brian McCaffrey for Matther Woodbury.

Mr. McCaffrey informed the Court that the parties were progressing toward mediation, and needed a continuance of 90 days to get the dispute mediated. Ms. Salassi agreed. The Court set September 23, 2016 as the deadline for pro bono mediation, with a status hearing re mediation set for October 24, 2016 at 9:00 am, and the hearing on the petition was continued to that date and time, in Dept. 204. The Court inquired whether any other dates need to be set at this time, and Ms. Salassi responded that her client was requesting the setting of a trial date at this time. Upon agreement of counsel, the matter is set for trial in Dept. 206 on December 9, 2016 at 9:30 am. A minute order referring the case to pro bono mediation is signed by the court. The parties agree to prepare and submit to the court a list of proposed witnesses for the trial.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Continued To Jul-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Matt Woodberry. Vernon Goins for Bruce Loughridge. Milla Lvovich for Charles Watts (Decedent's Brother) and Ricky Watts (Decedent's Nephew).

Ms. Lvovich stated that her Clients have not formerly entered this case, and that they want to file a petition to invalidate the Trust and request distribution to intestate heirs. Mr. Goins asked for more time to supplement the petition and for notice. Ms. Alavi stated that Mr. Loughridge has no authority, that he lacks standing, that the petition is procedurally defective and that she has obtained litigation Counsel. Ms. Alavi also raised the question of whether time has expired to contest the Trust. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust was continued to July 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Goins to get the petition procedurally in order and to respond with regard to the standing issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Jones. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Continued To May-13-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Matthew Woodberry (Trustee). Vernon Goins appeared by telephone for Bruce Loughridge.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of whether or not Mr. Loughridge has standing to bring this petition, whether or not Mr. Loughridge is a child of the decedent/related to the decedent, the issue of undue influence in preparation of the trust, whether the petition has merit, the petition not having been served on the two Beneficiaries (both represented by Daniel Conrad), etc. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust was continued to May 13, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for procedural aspects/service and to address the standing issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (65 Cleary Court, #8, San Francisco, Ca 94108)
Off Calendar Mar-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Matthew Woodbury (Trustee), Bruce Loughridge for and with Vernon Goins (Objector)

Matter called. Mr. Loughridge presented agrument in opposition to the sale stating that his client has a pending petition to determine heirship currently set for 3/25/14 and that his client is a tenant at the property. Ms. Alavi presented argument in support of the sale and requested that the Court proceed. The Court addressed the objections, overruled them, and proceeded with the sale. The sale was confirmed at $750,000.00 as overbid based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of
$717,000.00 is required.
